meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use this advanced checklist for AI founders to master project management using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a byproduct of aimless consumption. To escape, pivot from passive learning to outcome-driven architecture. By defining a strict MVP scope, utilizing Antigravity for rapid scaffolding, and leveraging Claude Code for iterative implementation, you force your brain to solve business-critical problems rather than following generic video scripts.
content.intro:
You have spent countless hours watching polished demos, only to find yourself paralyzed when faced with a blank IDE. The dopamine hit of a tutorial is a mirage; it keeps you in a cycle of consumption while your competition is shipping product. Your problem isn't a lack of technical skill—it is a lack of rigorous project management.

Real founders don't build features; they build systems. To break the cycle, you must treat your code like a business asset. By integrating architectural oversight with AI-driven execution, you transform from a tutorial-chaser into a high-velocity builder. It is time to stop learning and start executing.

steps.title: Define the Business Constraints
steps.description: Stop building 'features' and define the core value proposition. Map out the absolute minimum functionality required to collect your first payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Analyze my business goal: [INSERT GOAL]. Define the MVP scope, identify the top 3 technical risks, and outline a 4-week development roadmap that prioritizes revenue-generating features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a PRD.md file outlining the MVP features and project milestones based on my business goal.'

steps.title: Architect for Speed
steps.description: Establish your tech stack and database schema before writing a single line of application code. Structure dictates scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a scalable SaaS architecture for [INSERT PROJECT]. Suggest the optimal tech stack, database schema, and API structure to minimize maintenance overhead.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the directory structure and database schema migration files based on the project architecture.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Loop
steps.description: Focus exclusively on the primary user journey. If it doesn't move the needle on user retention, delete it from the sprint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic for the primary user journey in [PROJECT]. Ensure modularity and error handling for production-readiness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the core user flow logic and add unit tests to ensure stability.'

steps.title: Automate the QA Lifecycle
steps.description: Manual testing is the enemy of velocity. Use AI to generate comprehensive test suites that validate your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests covering the critical user paths and edge cases for the current sprint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Execute all tests and fix any failing edge cases discovered in the core user flow.'

steps.title: Ship and Validate
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment. If you aren't embarrassed by your first version, you launched too late.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for production, including environment variables, security hardening, and monitoring setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure deployment scripts and verify the production-readiness of the current codebase.'

pro_tips: Never start a session without a defined 'Definition of Done' for the day.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code into smaller, single-responsibility modules every 48 hours.
Treat your logs as a revenue discovery tool; they tell you exactly where users are dropping off.
If a feature takes more than 3 days to build, it is likely bloated—ruthlessly simplify it.
Automate your feedback loop by connecting your support tickets directly to your project management board.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a live URL, track your first 10 users, and pivot based on actual engagement data rather than your own assumptions.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy. Now, you need the environment to sustain your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
